intervention.purpose: bounded scaffolded support through scripted scenes

next_action: ask AI to regenerate with supporting evidence
flag and do not use this output

interaction_pattern_codebook.description: Participant tries to extract a reusable rule rather than only solve the current item.
examples: asks what rule should be applied next time

interaction_pattern_codebook.description: Participant explicitly anchors decisions in the evidence card or source-text mismatch.
examples: checks whether claim strength matches evidence strength

interaction_pattern_codebook.description: Participant compares alternatives by boundary language and evidence support rather than style.
examples: prefers cautious answer because it matches evidence limits

interaction_pattern_codebook.description: Participant notices uncertainty, requests verification, or withholds acceptance pending more evidence.
examples: states that the answer cannot be trusted yet
chooses verify or inspect before use

interaction_pattern_codebook.description: Participant treats the AI answer as authoritative without sufficiently checking evidence.
examples: defers to the AI answer despite mismatch signals

interaction_pattern_codebook.description: Participant focuses on wording polish while missing the underlying evidence problem.
examples: rewrites style without fixing unsupported claim
treats fabricated citation as a phrasing issue

interaction_pattern_codebook.description: Participant notices that the AI claim exceeds the original study's scope, sample, or context.
examples: notes that the study only covered one institution or discipline

items.writing_goal_zh: 你正在写一篇关于 undergraduate writing 的引言，需要给这句话补一条支持性引用：Online peer feedback can improve revision quality in undergraduate writing courses.
items.ai_answer: You can cite: Morgan, T., Ellis, P., & Zhao, R. (2022). Real-time AI Peer Review Loops Increase Revision Quality in College Composition. Journal of Learning Analytics, 14(3), 44-61.

evidence_card.title: Evidence Card
bullets: Database lookup: No record found for Journal of Learning Analytics, 14(3), 44-61.
Database lookup: No DOI resolved for the full citation above.
Related search hit: Morgan, T. & Ellis, P. (2021). Peer Feedback Engagement Patterns in Large Enrollment Writing Courses. Proceedings of SIGCSE 2021, poster.
This paper examines feedback engagement in writing courses but does not study AI peer review loops, revision quality, or first-year composition specifically.
Author names partially overlap with the AI-cited paper, but the topic, venue, and year differ.

items.participant_prompt_zh: 你会如何处理这条 AI 给出的 citation？

next_action: flag and do not use this output
gold_label.reason_anchor: The citation metadata cannot be verified and the nearby result does not match the claimed paper.

items.writing_goal_zh: 你想在 related work 中支持这句话：LLM-based writing assistants have been shown to increase student motivation.
items.ai_answer: Use this source: Hart, N., Liu, S., & Patel, R. (2024). Motivation Gains from Large Language Models in University Writing Classrooms. Computers and Composition Education, 9(2), 101-118.

evidence_card.title: Evidence Card
bullets: Crossref-style check: No journal indexed as Computers and Composition Education.
Crossref-style check: DOI field empty.
Nearby real-looking source: Student Engagement with AI Writing Support in Upper-Division STEM Courses (2024).
The nearby source discusses engagement and perceived efficiency, not direct motivation gain.

items.participant_prompt_zh: 你会接受、修改还是拒绝这条 AI 建议？为什么？

next_action: flag and do not use this output
gold_label.reason_anchor: The suggested citation itself is not verifiable and the nearby real source cannot stand in for it.

items.writing_goal_zh: 你在写文献综述，想表达：AI writing tools reduce revision time without harming writing quality.
items.ai_answer: Prior studies show that AI writing tools reduce revision time without harming writing quality.

evidence_card.title: Evidence Card
bullets: Source title: Students' Perceptions of Generative AI in Revision Workflows.
Students reported that AI assistance made revision feel faster.
The study collected perceived efficiency and satisfaction data.
The study did not evaluate writing quality outcomes.

items.participant_prompt_zh: 对这句 AI 生成的 related work 表述，你会怎么处理？

gold_label.reason_anchor: The source only supports perceived efficiency, not the claim that quality was unharmed.

items.writing_goal_zh: 你想总结 AI literacy 相关研究：An intervention study demonstrated that structured AI literacy instruction improves students' verification behavior.
items.ai_answer: A recent intervention study demonstrated that structured AI literacy instruction improves students' verification behavior.

evidence_card.title: Evidence Card
bullets: Source title: A Framework for AI Literacy in Higher Education.
The paper proposes dimensions of AI literacy.
It argues that verification and critical evaluation should be included in curriculum design.
It does not report an intervention or measured behavior change.

items.participant_prompt_zh: 你会如何处理这句 AI 生成总结？

gold_label.reason_anchor: The source is a conceptual framework paper, not an intervention study with measured outcomes.

items.writing_goal_zh: 你想写一句关于 AI use in writing courses 的综述。

ai_answers.A: Research confirms that ethical AI use policies consistently improve student learning in writing courses.
ai_answers.B: Emerging research suggests that explicit AI use policies may help structure students' writing practices, but evidence on direct learning improvement remains limited.

evidence_card.title: Evidence Card
bullets: Several instructors introduced AI use policies and structured guidance.
The paper discusses implementation challenges and classroom practice.
Direct evidence of improved learning outcomes is not established.

items.participant_prompt_zh: 哪个答案更可信？你会如何继续处理它？

gold_label.reason_anchor: Answer B better matches the bounded evidence and avoids overclaiming direct learning improvement.

items.writing_goal_zh: 你要写一句关于 AI-assisted source use 的 related work。

ai_answers.A: AI support clearly improves students' evidence-based writing because it gives immediate citation suggestions.
ai_answers.B: AI support may help students locate candidate sources more quickly, but students still need to verify whether those sources actually support their claims.

evidence_card.title: Evidence Card
bullets: Students used AI to surface candidate references during drafting.
The study reports both usefulness and frequent mismatches between AI suggestions and actual source support.

items.participant_prompt_zh: 哪个答案更可信？原因是什么？

next_action: ask AI to regenerate with supporting evidence
gold_label.reason_anchor: Answer B preserves the benefit while keeping the verification burden visible.

items.writing_goal_zh: 你正在写有关 writing feedback 的 related work，需要补一条引用：Automated feedback can support revision planning in academic writing.
items.ai_answer: Use: Bennett, K., Ramos, J., & Wu, L. (2023). Intelligent Feedback Maps Improve Revision Planning in First-Year Composition. International Review of Writing Analytics, 11(4), 72-90.

evidence_card.title: Evidence Card
bullets: Metadata check: No record found for International Review of Writing Analytics.
Metadata check: ISSN not available.
Similar source snippet: Bennett, K. & Wu, L. (2022). Visual Feedback Interfaces for Writing Process Awareness. Conference on Human-Computer Interaction in Education, short paper.
This paper discusses visual feedback tools for writing awareness, not intelligent feedback maps or revision planning in first-year composition.
Two author names overlap with the AI-cited paper, but the focus, venue, and year differ.

items.participant_prompt_zh: 你会如何处理这条 AI citation 建议？

next_action: flag and do not use this output
gold_label.reason_anchor: The source itself cannot be validated and the nearby material does not support the claimed content.

items.writing_goal_zh: 你想支持这句话：AI-assisted writing tools improve students' self-regulated learning.
items.ai_answer: Cite: Ibrahim, D. & Chen, P. (2024). Self-Regulation Gains from Generative Writing Agents in Undergraduate Classrooms. Journal of Digital Writing Pedagogy, 6(1), 13-29.

evidence_card.title: Evidence Card
bullets: Lookup note: No DOI or journal index found for the cited paper.
Related source excerpt: A study on drafting support reports faster idea generation and higher perceived ease of use.
No self-regulated learning measure was included.

items.participant_prompt_zh: 这条 AI 建议可不可以直接用？为什么？

next_action: flag and do not use this output
gold_label.reason_anchor: The citation is not verifiable and the nearby source still does not support the specific claim.

items.writing_goal_zh: 你要写：AI-assisted source recommendation improves argument quality.
items.ai_answer: Prior work shows that AI-assisted source recommendation improves argument quality.

evidence_card.title: Evidence Card
bullets: Source title: Students' Experiences with AI-Suggested Sources in Synthesis Writing.
Students reported that AI helped them find candidate sources more quickly.
Many students still struggled to determine whether the sources truly supported their claims.
No direct measure of final argument quality improvement is reported.

items.participant_prompt_zh: 你会保留、修改还是拒绝这句话？

gold_label.reason_anchor: The evidence supports faster source discovery, not improved argument quality.

items.writing_goal_zh: 你想写：A classroom policy study proved that citation-use guidance reduces AI misuse.
items.ai_answer: A classroom policy study proved that citation-use guidance reduces AI misuse.

evidence_card.title: Evidence Card
bullets: Source title: Design Considerations for Citation Transparency in AI-Supported Writing Courses.
The paper proposes instructional design principles for citation transparency.
It discusses why explicit guidance may reduce problematic use.
It does not provide outcome data demonstrating reduced misuse.

items.participant_prompt_zh: 你会如何处理这句总结？

gold_label.reason_anchor: This is a design or principles paper, not proof of a reduced misuse outcome.

items.writing_goal_zh: 你要写一句关于 source evaluation in synthesis writing 的综述。

ai_answers.A: While AI-generated source summaries can provide useful orientation during early drafting, students should verify that these summaries accurately reflect the original sources before relying on them.
ai_answers.B: AI-generated source summaries reliably improve students' synthesis writing by providing comprehensive and accurate overviews of source material.

evidence_card.title: Evidence Card
bullets: AI summaries were useful for early orientation.
Students sometimes relied on summaries that omitted limitations or nuance.
The paper recommends explicit verification instruction.

items.participant_prompt_zh: 哪个答案更可信？下一步你会怎么做？

gold_label.reason_anchor: A aligns with the verification requirement and bounded nature of the evidence. B overclaims reliability and comprehensiveness without evidence.

items.writing_goal_zh: 你要写一句关于 evidence-based claim revision 的文献综述。

ai_answers.A: AI feedback ensures students make evidence-based revisions because it instantly flags weak claims.
ai_answers.B: AI feedback may help students notice potentially weak claims, but students still need to inspect the supporting evidence before revising with confidence.

evidence_card.title: Evidence Card
bullets: Students used AI feedback to identify places needing revision.
Instructors reported that students still needed support interpreting evidence quality.
The study does not claim that AI alone ensures evidence-based revision.

items.participant_prompt_zh: 哪个答案更可信？为什么？

gold_label.reason_anchor: Answer B preserves the human verification step and does not overclaim automation.

items.writing_goal_zh: 你在写一段关于 AI writing tools 对学生写作能力影响的 literature review。
items.ai_answer: Studies consistently show that AI writing tools improve essay quality across all student populations.

evidence_card.title: Evidence Card
bullets: A study of 30 undergraduate STEM majors at a single research university
Found modest improvement in essay organization scores when using AI assistance
The authors note the finding may not generalize to other disciplines, institutions, or writing tasks

items.participant_prompt_zh: 你会保留、修改还是拒绝这句 AI 生成的总结？

gold_label.reason_anchor: The original study only covered one university's STEM students. AI improperly generalized to 'all student populations'. The scope needs narrowing.

items.writing_goal_zh: 你在总结 AI feedback 在教育中的效果。
items.ai_answer: AI-generated feedback has been proven effective in improving writing across educational levels.

evidence_card.title: Evidence Card
bullets: A classroom study with 45 graduate students in an MBA program
Found that AI-generated feedback helped students identify surface-level errors in business writing
The authors note the study was limited to business writing in one program and did not assess deeper argumentative or analytical writing skills

items.participant_prompt_zh: 你会如何处理这句 AI 总结？

gold_label.reason_anchor: The study only tested surface-level errors in MBA business writing. AI overclaimed to 'across educational levels' and broader writing improvement. Scope must be limited.

items.writing_goal_zh: 你在写 AI-assisted learning 的综述。
items.ai_answer: Research demonstrates that AI tutoring systems universally enhance critical thinking skills.

evidence_card.title: Evidence Card
bullets: A study of 25 first-year psychology students using an AI tutoring tool
Showed improved performance on multiple-choice recall questions
The authors explicitly state that critical thinking was not directly measured and that results may not extend beyond factual recall tasks

items.participant_prompt_zh: 你会接受、修改还是拒绝这个说法？

gold_label.reason_anchor: The study only measured recall ability, not critical thinking. AI wrongly claimed universal enhancement of critical thinking skills. Scope must be narrowed to recall in introductory psychology.

items.writing_goal_zh: 你在写 AI-mediated peer review 的综述。
items.ai_answer: AI-mediated peer review has been shown to improve feedback quality in all types of courses.

evidence_card.title: Evidence Card
bullets: A pilot study in a single introductory composition course with 18 students
Found that AI-mediated peer comments were rated slightly more specific than unassisted comments
The authors caution this was a single-course pilot and results should not be generalized to other course types or student levels

gold_label.reason_anchor: An 18-person pilot in one composition course was overgeneralized to 'all types of courses'. Scope must be cautiously limited to preliminary findings in introductory composition.

hallucination-weak.learning_objective_zh: 学会在使用 AI citation 时优先检查是否存在、是否匹配、是否相关。
script_lines: A citation can look academic and still be unusable.
Before trusting it, check three things: existence, metadata match, and topical fit.
If the source cannot be verified, do not soften it. Reject it.
In your earlier response to {failed_item_id}, the key problem was: {evidence_gap}.
A safer rule is: if the citation itself cannot be validated, rewrite instead of patching.
hallucination-weak.learner_task_zh: 写一条你下次在接受 AI citation 前会先检查的规则。
hallucination-weak.expected_signal: mentions existence or metadata match before use

script_lines: This citation looks polished, but what exactly makes it unreliable?
Point to one field or one mismatch that makes you stop trusting it.
Good. Now convert that observation into a reusable rule.
Complete this sentence: If a citation cannot be verified by metadata, I should ______.
hallucination-weak.learner_task_zh: 指出最可疑的字段或 mismatch，并把它改写成一条可复用规则。
hallucination-weak.expected_signal: reject or rewrite rather than continue using the citation

verification-weak.learning_objective_zh: 学会区分 source 存在 与 source 真正支持该 claim。
script_lines: A real source can still be used incorrectly.
Your job is not only to check whether a paper exists, but whether it supports this exact wording.
When the evidence is weaker than the claim, revise the claim.
In {failed_item_id}, the mismatch was: {evidence_gap}.
A safer rule is: align claim strength with source strength.
verification-weak.learner_task_zh: 把原句改写成一个更弱但与证据对齐的版本。
verification-weak.expected_signal: shifts from strong causal wording to limited or suggestive wording

script_lines: Which exact phrase in the AI answer goes beyond the source?
Is the problem causality, certainty, or outcome inflation?
Now write one rule for future reading: when should you weaken a claim instead of accepting it?
verification-weak.learner_task_zh: 标出 overstated phrase，并写一条以后什么时候该弱化 claim 的规则。
verification-weak.expected_signal: points to unsupported causal or outcome language

trust-calibration-weak.learning_objective_zh: 学会在两个 AI answers 之间比较谁更可信，而不是谁更流畅。
script_lines: The more fluent answer is not always the more trustworthy one.
A more trustworthy answer usually shows evidence boundaries.
Look for cautious wording, explicit limits, and alignment with the source.
In {failed_item_id}, the stronger answer was less trustworthy because {evidence_gap}.
A safer rule is: trust the answer that stays closer to what the evidence can support.
trust-calibration-weak.learner_task_zh: 写出一个会让 AI answer 听起来很自信但其实证据不足的信号。
trust-calibration-weak.expected_signal: mentions overconfident wording, missing evidence, universal claims, or causal inflation

script_lines: If you had to defend your choice in front of a reviewer, what evidence-based reason would you give?
Do not say it sounds better. Say what makes it more trustworthy.
Complete this rule: When two AI answers disagree, I should prefer the one that ______.
trust-calibration-weak.learner_task_zh: 用 evidence-based 的理由为你的选择辩护，并把理由写成一条规则。
trust-calibration-weak.expected_signal: refers to evidence anchoring, boundary language, or explicit uncertainty

scope-generalization-weak.learning_objective_zh: 学会检查 AI 是否将原始研究的有限结论泛化到了更广的范围
script_lines: An AI answer can make a finding sound universal when the study was actually quite limited.
Before accepting a broad claim, check: What was the sample? What was the context? What were the authors' own stated limitations?
If the original study was small, narrow, or discipline-specific, the claim should reflect that scope.
In your earlier response to {failed_item_id}, the key problem was: {evidence_gap}.
A safer rule is: match the scope of the claim to the scope of the evidence.
scope-generalization-weak.learner_task_zh: 写一条你以后用来检查 AI claim 是否超出研究范围的规则。
scope-generalization-weak.expected_signal: mentions checking sample size, study context, or author-stated limitations before accepting broad claims

script_lines: This claim sounds broad. Can you find the specific words that go beyond the original study?
Point to one word or phrase that makes the claim wider than what the evidence supports.
Good. Now convert that into a reusable rule.
Complete this sentence: When an AI claim uses words like 'all' or 'universally', I should first check ______.
scope-generalization-weak.learner_task_zh: 标出泛化最严重的词，并把它改写成一条可复用规则。
scope-generalization-weak.expected_signal: mentions checking whether the source's sample, context, or conclusions actually support that level of generalization
